llmResponse.agendaChanges: Item 8.2 (Henry Ford School Presentation and SPSA Approval) was removed from the agenda at the start of the meeting. The Pledge of Allegiance (Section 2) was noted as not happening (no student-led pledge). Welcome remarks were delivered by the president during Call to Order/opening. All other items proceeded in agenda order.

items.title: Call to Order
Roll Call
Welcome / Pledge of Allegiance
Changes to the Agenda
Approval of the Agenda (Action Required)
Public Comment

publicComments.name: Pam Barash
publicComments.summary: A kindergarten teacher at Henry Ford School spoke about the rising cost of living in the Bay Area, arguing that teachers deserve compensation sufficient to afford to live near where they teach, in order to attract and retain quality educators.

publicComments.name: Bree Henney
publicComments.summary: A Clifford parent expressed outrage about a teacher with a history of inappropriate conduct being hired at the school, demanded full transparency from the district about its hiring practices and safeguards, and called for accountability.

publicComments.name: Sarah Heder-Spack
publicComments.summary: A Clifford parent of two students questioned how the district found it acceptable to hire an accused sexual harasser, called for a retroactive review of hiring across the district under AB 2534, and said community trust had been shattered.

publicComments.name: Josh Levinson
publicComments.summary: A Clifford parent of three students said he filed a formal Title IX complaint after learning from his son about ongoing concerning behavior by the teacher, and urged the board to acknowledge the issue is not merely historical and commit to transparency and long-term change.

publicComments.name: Jonathan Cantu
publicComments.summary: Asked via Zoom whether the board finds it acceptable to employ a teacher fired for inappropriately touching students, and what action the board will take if not.

items.title: Labor Association Comments

publicComments.name: Shauna Ingersoll
publicComments.summary: Chapter 5 President announced a $100 scholarship for an outstanding 8th grade graduating student, reminded principals of the submission deadline, and invited classified staff to a Classified Appreciation Week dinner on May 16th.
